# Aksara Bali Converter

> Konverter Latin ke Aksara Bali | Latin to Balinese Script Converter

A free, open-source web application for converting Latin text to traditional Balinese script (Aksara Bali) with comprehensive Sanskrit support, educational tools, and cultural preservation features.

## Site Information

- **URL**: https://transliterasi-latin-ke-bahasa-bali.vercel.app
- **Author**: Doni Wirawan
- **Language**: Indonesian (id), English (en)
- **License**: MIT
- **Last Updated**: 2026-03-27

## Pages

### Core Pages
- `/` — Main Latin to Balinese script converter (real-time conversion)
- `/practice` — Interactive learning: quiz mode, writing canvas with hand gesture recognition, virtual Balinese keyboard
- `/blog` — Articles about Balinese script, history, culture, and linguistics
- `/faq` — Frequently asked questions about Balinese script and the tool
- `/admin` — Admin dashboard (authentication required)

### Blog Articles
- `/blog/mengenal-aksara-bali` — History and structure of Balinese script
- `/blog/cara-belajar-aksara-bali` — Beginner's guide to learning Balinese script
- `/blog/aksara-bali-dan-bahasa-sansekerta` — Relationship between Balinese script and Sanskrit
- `/blog/lontar-naskah-kuno-bali` — Lontar palm leaf manuscripts and cultural preservation
- `/blog/perbedaan-aksara-bali-jawa-latin` — Comparison of Balinese, Javanese, and Latin scripts
- `/blog/upaya-pelestarian-aksara-bali-digital` — Digital preservation of Balinese script

## Key Features

- Real-time Latin to Balinese Unicode script (U+1B00–U+1B7F) conversion
- Sanskrit word recognition (100+ common words) with automatic murda (retroflex) forms
- V=W phonological equivalency support
- Interactive quiz with easy/medium/hard difficulty levels
- Hand gesture drawing recognition using MediaPipe (webcam-based)
- Virtual Balinese keyboard for direct script input
- PWA (Progressive Web App) — installable, works offline
- Bilingual UI: Indonesian and English
- Dark mode support
- Supabase analytics backend (conversion logs, quiz results, writing checks)

## Technical Stack

- **Framework**: Next.js 15 (Pages Router)
- **Frontend**: React 19, Bootstrap 5, Lucide React
- **Backend**: Supabase (PostgreSQL + Auth)
- **Deployment**: Vercel
- **Script**: Unicode Balinese (U+1B00–U+1B7F)
- **Font**: Noto Sans Balinese (Google Fonts)

## Balinese Script Facts

Balinese script (Aksara Bali) is a traditional Brahmic abugida used in Bali, Indonesia. Each consonant inherently carries the vowel /a/, with other vowels written as diacritical marks (pangangge). The script has 47 base consonants, 8 independent vowels, and uses virama (adeg-adeg) for consonant clusters. It was added to Unicode in version 5.0 (2006) at block U+1B00–U+1B7F.
